installation: Primary: shadcn CLI against the ReactBits registry — `npx shadcn@latest add https://reactbits.dev/r/<Name>-<TS|JS>-<TW|CSS>` (verified working, returns registry-item JSON; e.g. `npx shadcn@latest add https://reactbits.dev/r/SplitText-TS-TW`). Namespaced form after adding `"@react-bits": "https://reactbits.dev/r/{name}"` to components.json registries: `npx shadcn@latest add @react-bits/BlurText-TS-TW`. Alternative: jsrepo CLI — `npx jsrepo add https://reactbits.dev/<ts|js>/<tailwind|css>/<Category>/<Component>` (e.g. .../ts/tailwind/TextAnimations/SplitText). Manual copy-paste from each doc page also supported. Every component ships 4 variants: JS-CSS, JS-TW, TS-CSS, TS-TW. For this Astro+Tailwind v4 blog use the TS-TW variants.
dependencies: gsap@^3.13.0 (SplitText, AnimatedContent, FadeContent, TextType, ScrollReveal/Float, Shuffle, ScrambledText, DotGrid, Masonry, MagicBento, PillNav, StaggeredMenu, FlowingMenu, CardNav, BounceCards)

motion@^12.23.12 (BlurText, ShinyText, GradientText, RotatingText, DecryptedText, TrueFocus, VariableProximity, CountUp, Counter, AnimatedList, Dock, TiltedCard, Carousel, Stack, Stepper, ScrollVelocity)
ogl@^1.0.11 (only for WebGL backgrounds: Aurora, SoftAurora, DarkVeil, Particles, Grainient, Threads, FaultyTerminal, SpecularButton)
react-router-dom@^6.30.1 (PillNav as shipped — strip it and use <a> in Astro)
zero-dep components need nothing: ClickSpark, Noise, SpotlightCard, GlitchText, StarBorder, GlareHover, Magnet, GradualBlur, BorderGlow, GooeyNav, LogoLoop, LetterGlitch, Waves, ProfileCard, Folder
tailwindVersion: TW variants are pure utility-class components with no plugin or tailwind.config requirement — they work as-is on Tailwind v4 (and v3). Exception: a few components reference custom animation utilities (verified in registry source: StarBorder uses animate-star-movement-top/bottom, GlitchText uses animate-glitch-before/after) — in v4 define those keyframes+utilities in your global CSS via @theme/@keyframes (docs provide the snippet; in v3 it went in tailwind.config). If you want zero Tailwind coupling for any component, the -CSS variants ship plain CSS instead.

components.name: Split Text
components.blogUse: Homepage hero title — staggered per-character/word entrance on load (gsap + @gsap/react, ScrollTrigger)

components.name: Blur Text
components.blogUse: Hero subtitle and section headings — blur-to-crisp reveal on viewport entry (motion, IntersectionObserver)

components.name: Shiny Text
components.blogUse: Subtle metallic sheen on nav links, 'Read more' links, newsletter CTA label — very at home on dark (motion)

components.name: Glitch Text
components.blogUse: 404 page headline — CSS-only RGB-split glitch, zero deps (needs custom animate-glitch-* keyframes in Tailwind v4 CSS)

components.name: Gradient Text
components.blogUse: Single accent word in the hero headline — keep to one accent hue for the Swiss look (motion)

components.name: Text Type
components.blogUse: Typewriter hero tagline alternative (gsap)

components.name: Rotating Text
components.blogUse: Hero rotating keyword ('I write about X / Y / Z') (motion)

components.name: Decrypted Text

components.name: Count Up
components.blogUse: About page stats (posts written, years) and tags page post counts (motion)

components.name: Scroll Reveal

components.name: Scroll Float

notes: 1) Docs site is fully client-rendered (SPA) — the reliable machine-readable source is the shadcn registry at https://reactbits.dev/r/<Name>-<TS|JS>-<TW|CSS> (verified; returns registry-item JSON with code + dependencies). 2) SSR/Astro: all components are client-side animated React; browser APIs (canvas, WebGL, IntersectionObserver, ResizeObserver) are inside useEffect so they don't crash SSR, but every one must be a hydrated island (client:load for above-the-fold hero text, client:visible for backgrounds/cards). Backgrounds render nothing until hydration — give containers a fallback bg color to avoid flash. 3) React 19: components are plain function components; gsap@3.13 + @gsap/react@2.1 and motion@12 both support React 19 — no known blockers. 4) PillNav (a 'must') imports react-router-dom's Link — replace with <a> for Astro and drop that dep. 5) Two animation libraries coexist: gsap (~23 components) and motion (~16); expect both in the bundle if you mix — that's fine but prefer picking per-page. 6) OGL WebGL backgrounds (Aurora, DarkVeil, Particles, Grainient, Threads, SoftAurora, FaultyTerminal) add ~50KB and a GPU canvas — use at most one per page with client:visible. 7) three.js/@react-three/fiber components (Silk, Beams, Ballpit, Hyperspeed, Dither, ASCIIText, ModelViewer, Lanyard, FluidGlass, PixelBlast) and matter-js (FallingText) are heavy — all marked skip. 8) StarBorder and GlitchText require custom keyframes/utilities added to Tailwind v4 CSS (@theme + @keyframes; names: star-movement-top/bottom, glitch-before/after). 9) No built-in prefers-reduced-motion handling in most components — gate island hydration or pass reduced props yourself. 10) An official MCP server exists (docs at https://reactbits.dev/get-started/mcp) if you want in-editor component fetching.
